ogTitle: Trundle vs Trungley Hall: Climate Safety

heading: Trundle vs Trungley Hall — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Trundle, New South Wales and Trungley Hall, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Trundle has a lower overall climate risk score (38/100) compared to Trungley Hall (39/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Trundle or Trungley Hall safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Trundle has a flood risk score of 54/100, while Trungley Hall scores 32/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Trundle scores 34/100 for bushfire risk. Trungley Hall scores 59/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Trundle or Trungley Hall?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Trundle has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.33% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
